en/the-peace-of-mind.title: THE PEACE OF MIND
en/the-peace-of-mind.body:
Living in the life some people aimlessly singing, some people wandering aimlessly, some people binge drinking, some people spent themselves on making money, earning fame... and he, what is he looking for?!

Once, the clumsy ego uttered that self-confident question, and then repeated it until one day, the answer begins to be reveal, which is also the day Kien Binh Minh Company was established and the day the journey of "Haptic Eyeglasses" begin to reach the blind community.

 

Giving birth is not as hard as nurturing, but even with constant questioning, Mr. Nguyen Huu Qui - founder of Kien Binh Minh - still smiles and answers softly: "His mother could not see in the past" and up until now, he still answers the same way when every time someone asks.

Gather up a little yellow of sunshine
Lighting up life to relieve the pain

As the journey of Kien Binh Minh begins, more than a thousand of "Haptics eyeglasses" travel all over the provinces of Viet Nam to the blind community. The eyeglasses had gone through nine different versions with the initial price of the product was at a miracle of 850 usd, but now, it has been made much more approachable to support the blind community in Vietnam.

As people say: "Sadness when shared, it will be lessened, joy when shared, it will be doubled". Mr. Qui has been walking silently with the "Haptic eyeglasses" all these years. He empathizes the disadvantages in the lives of many others, understanding them in a broad sense, and more or less bringing the dawn to the unfortunate fates. The number of people he has helped, nobody knows. Many newspapers, radio, and television ask for interviews but he often rejects. Figures do not matter but the important point is that his care for the community can be viewed from different perspectives. He makes sacrifices, and pursues his willingness to help the community. And as a person of Christianity, his work exhibits charity, altruism, and religious belief.

Gently, he speaks to everyone, "It has been accomplished." It is the day when the Prime Minister decides to "order" 300,000 eyeglasses and gives free to the blind community in Vietnam.

The phrase "It has been accomplished", beside the meaning of social service, it also carries a meaning of paying off a debt each of us has enjoyed and gained from the society. Kien Binh Minh now has transferred the entire "Haptic eyeglasses" project to Ho Chi Minh University of Technology and Education to continue its development. The Ministry of Science and Technology and the University have signed an independent contract on a national level with the aim to research and complete the design, build electronic Haptic eyeglasses that can protect the blind from obstacles. The total expense for the development project is estimated to be around 5.57 billion VND. In the near future, "Haptic eyeglasses" will no longer have physical wires and can make phone calls, can help the blind see color, and able to listen to music....

Although with the phrase "It has been accomplished", from the eyes of Mr. Qui, many questions, desires, and passions... still emerge with new ideas, a renewal, a new beginning for the cause of social service. Life is a collision and we are entitled to doubt. A swallow cannot create spring, but it can make a life to remember...

Personally, I find something really interesting: doing Charity is harder than doing Business. Therefore, I would like to dedicate to Mr. Qui, as well as the social workers, the poet of Emily Dickinson's "Not in Vain" :

If I could stop a despairing soul

I will live a useful life

If I could make the pain go away

Or ease someone's sadness

If I could comfort the thin, pale Nightingale

Until the day I can return to my home

I will live a life worth living.

en/journey-visit-the-ethnicity-of-chut-h.title: JOURNEY: VISIT THE ETHNICITY OF CHUT-HUONG KHE, HA TINH
en/journey-visit-the-ethnicity-of-chut-h.body:
02/12/2022: The journey begins for members of the Kien Vuong delegation from the two regions of the country to the Central region, where the land is dry in summer and stormy in winter. The place has the harshest climate, often suffers from natural disasters, storms and floods all year round. Where Kien Vuong has many times accompanied local mass organizations to carry out charity programs. And the journey to visit the Chut ethnic people this time too, the warmth and the most meaningful gifts of Kien Vuong were shared.

Both Kien Vuong Hanoi and Ho Chi Minh met in Vinh City, Nghe An in cold and cloudy weather, before moving to Huong Khe town, Ha Tinh province

03/12/2022: The small zigzag road from the center of Huong Khe district, Ha Tinh province to Rao Tre village (Huong Lien commune) is about 20km long. Through many ramps, looming along the roadside are houses in the middle of Ka Day mountain, Group Kien Vuong arrived at the village of Chut ethnic minority.

Although they have been encouraged by the border guards to migrate to more spacious houses, some Chut people still maintain the habit of living in their old residence near the forest.

 

Coming to the Chut ethnic group, where 31 years ago (1991) in a cave on the Truong Son mountain range of the Vietnam-Laos border, the border guards discovered that there were only 20 Chut people living. An ethnic group that almost went extinct, so far it has grown to 46 households in Rao Tre village, Huong Lien commune, Huong Khe district, Ha Tinh province.

According to officials from the provincial Youth Union, the situation of inbreeding marriage has decreased significantly but still exists; Along with the difficulties in daily life, children of Chut ethnic group will also be much smaller than ordinary children. 

 

The Chut people have 2 Tet holidays in a year, this is the most unique activity of the Chut people: Tet Lap Lo on the 7th day of the 7th lunar month, meaning "plugging holes, sowing seeds", signaling completion of sowing and pruning. On the fields and the Cham Cha Boi New Year on the 12th day of the 11th lunar month, the meaning is the New Year of rice, held to give thanks to heaven and earth for the favorable rain and wind, bountiful crops, and proliferating children. and welcome the new season. With the desire to bring warmth and meaningful gifts to help the Chut ethnic people welcome the Cham Cha Boi New Year with more warmth and joy, at 9:00 a.m. on December 3, Kien Vuong cooperated with Ha Tinh Provincial Youth Union to present 34 gifts for preschool children and primary school students, and 46 gifts for households of Chut ethnic group. The gifts include: warm clothes, warm blankets, notebooks, confectionery, rice, monosodium glutamate, cooking oil, fish sauce and monosodium glutamate.

At the program, the Organizing Committee presented 46 gifts to households.

 

The program also awarded 52 gifts to students of Chut ethnic group

en/gift-program-for-ethnic-minorities-in.title: GIFT PROGRAM FOR ETHNIC MINORITIES IN DON DUONG - LAM DONG
en/gift-program-for-ethnic-minorities-in.body:
So after more than two years of being affected by Covid-19, Kien Vuong's love-sharing journey continued to roll to remote areas with many difficulties in need of the help of community. On July 24, Kien Vuong coordinated with the Youth Union of Lam Dong Provincial Police to organize a program to give gifts to disadvantaged ethnic minority households in Tu Tra commune, Don Duong district.

Kien Vuong group presented at the gift gathering point early in the morning to distribute the gifts. Each person single-handedly prepared 200 gifts including rice, cooking oil, soup powder, confectionery... for disadvantaged households in Tu Tra commune

People and children gathered at the village hall before the time

Tu Tra commune is an ethnic minority commune of  Don Duong district. The majority are C'ho, Chill and ChRu ethnic groups. People in the commune mainly live on agriculture (growing rice, coffee, crops, raising cattle and poultry) and fishing in streams and rivers... depends largely on nature. Life is full of poverty and hardship. The destination this time of the Kien Vuong delegation is the two hamlets of Ro Lom and Hawai, located nearly 20 kilometers from the center of the district, the road to the village, although there are still many sections that have not been concreted, the road surface is concave and slippery, the rainy day is more and more slippery and muddy. But in general, it was somewhat more spacious than the roads leading to the highland villages that Kien Vuong used to go through.

With the support of the Executive Committee of the Provincial Public Security Youth Union, the Executive Committee of the Provincial Public Security Women's Union, the Executive Committee of the PX03 Division and the local Red Cross Society, the gift giving program was carried out quickly.

The program that Kien Vuong joined with the Youth Union of Lam Dong Provincial Police this time is part of the Green March campaign in 2022, working together for the community and welcoming the 12th Congress of the Ho Chi Minh Communist Youth Union of Lam Dong Province Public Security. The program gave 200 gifts to households in difficult circumstances, each containing 10kg of rice, necessities and confectionery...

Besides, knowing that due to the difficult economic situation, many households are not eligible for periodic health check-ups, especially during the current complicated epidemic period. Kien Vuong also donated some necessary medicines to the family medicine cabinet for backup in urgent cases.

Hopefully, with the companionship and support of the Youth Union of Lam Dong Provincial Police, it has brought people in Tu Tra commune a happy Sunday, and more motivation to strive to build a better life. .

en/1001-warm-cloths-come-to-the-children.title: 1001 WARM CLOTHS COME TO THE CHILDREN IN DIEN BIEN
en/1001-warm-cloths-come-to-the-children.body:
Another winter is approaching, continuing on the journey to bring warmth to children in the highland, the next stop of Kien Vuong this time is Dien Bien - which is considered the "poor navel" of the country. According to 2016 data, Dien Bien province has the highest poverty rate in the country with 44.82%. People's lives, which are already in poverty, are even more difficult when they have to bear the heavy consequences of terrible floods. Tuan Giao is a mountainous district of Dien Bien province, about 450km from Hanoi. According to the narration of Mr. Do Van Nhat (a member of Kien Vuong delegation), to get to Tuan Giao, you have to cross a bumpy and difficult pass with a cliff on one side and a deep abyss on the other. The people here are mainly Thai ethnic people, living by farming on the hillsides and cliffs. That's why a year-round life with enough food and clothing is already lucky, so the children's education has not been paid enough attention. The conditions for the children to go to school are therefore more difficult, not to mention the school's facilities are still lacking. Most of the children learn just to know the letters. Many children study at middle school have to drop out of school midway. Teachers have to regularly go from house to house to convince their parents for their children to continue going to school.

The pictures cannot describe the hardships of the children in the highlands, but only when coming to the place can we truly feel that difficulty. For Mr. Nhat, although this is the 3rd time he has accompanied Mr.Qui and Ms. Hien on a volunteer journey, this trip still leaves him with many indescribable impressions and emotions. "Knowing that there are still many difficult situations and needing help, however, helping some children who are still going to school in the whole Quai Nua commune made the whole group feel very relieved. A whole package of gifts was carefully sorted in 3 hours with the help of many newcomers and send it to the children with the utmost care. All 3 schools in the commune are very happy, even the parents. The clothes are new so everyone likes it, the day of the gift giving is like a festival for children and parents." - Mr.Nhat shares.

With the help of Dien Bien Provincial Youth Union, on October 28, Kien Vuong gives warm sleeves and plastic slippers to 1,011 students at 3 schools: Quai Nua Secondary School, Quai Nua Primary School, and Quai Nua Primary School. School No. 2 Quai Nua, Tuan Giao district. With the desire to somewhat alleviate the severe cold of the border winter, giving them strength to go to school with a peace of mind, in addition to warm clothes, Kien Vuong also brings 320 warm blankets and 320 single curtains, beside that, Kien Vuong also supports the school with some necessities. Hopefully these will be practical gifts for the children in the coming winter. Saying goodbye to Tuan Giao, Kien Vuong continues on the journey to many other difficult schools across the country to share with students a hard part of life.

Here are some pictures of the gift giving ceremony in Quai Nua commune, Tuan Giao district, Dien Bien:

en/the-last-leg-of-the-journey-bringing-.title: THE LAST LEG OF THE JOURNEY BRINGING WARMTH TO THE CHILDREN OF YEN BAI HIGHLAND
en/the-last-leg-of-the-journey-bringing-.body:
With an altitude of about 1000m above sea level, to get to Mu Cang Chai, we have to go through Khau Pha pass - one of the four great peaks of the Northwest Pass. From the center of Mu Cang Chai town, the road leading up to Lao Chai commune is winding and embracing the wind-swept waists, the higher we go, the more we can feel the bitter cold of the highland winter. Following the footsteps of teacher Duong Thi Lieu - Vice Principal of Lao Chai Secondary School for Ethnic Minorities, the group reaches the second school site on the journey to bring warmth to children in Yen Bai upland.

 

 

First impression is the politeness of the students. When seeing strangers arrive, they constantly nod and say hello, but as soon as they see the camera lens, they quickly turn away. According to the teacher, just a few days before the gifts were delivered, the children were very excited and anxious until the day the group come to visit. While the group are talking to the teachers to learn more about the teaching and learning conditions of the teachers and students, the children gather in the middle of the school yard. Some male students are busy helping the teachers to carry the confectionery, clothes. The atmosphere is bustling, the sound of children playing and calling each other echoes throughout the mountains and forests.

Overall, the school looks spacious, but the facilities, classrooms and living conditions of the teachers and students here are still difficult

Because the outdoor temperature is decreasing while the children are not dressed warm enough, the group quickly distributes the gifts for the children so they can return to their boarding houses, and some of the children going to class because it is their "shift". The school looks spacious from the outside, but the reality of the classrooms is still lacking. Students have to divide their shifts to study,and the teachers here have to use their personal money for construction and for their personal uses. Like a large yard that has just been poured with solid and clean concrete, it is also the work of the teachers and with the help from the parents as well. Ms. Lieu jokes: "The teachers here are both teachers and builders!"

Ms. Phung, on behalf of the charity group, gives a hot and cold water tower to the teachers

Crossing the steep road, both sides of the forest peach trees are in full bloom, we follow the students to the boarding house a few hundred meters away. It is said to be a model of a boarding house, but in fact, they live almost entirely at the school. The whole school currently has 8 boarding rooms, each room is about 50 square meters, no windows, dark, and only the door has light. Pile of clothes to dry, squeezed on steel wire. 25 bunk beds are set closely together. This is both a sleeping place, a place to play, and a place to study for more than 50 children. To move, one person must sit on the bed to make way for the other person to come through. The girls' housing is no better either. Priority is given to the 2-storey building, but the building is also deteriorating. Walls and ceilings are peeling off piece by piece. There is a bed, an aluminum box for storing things, and clothes covered on the iron bars of the balcony behind the house. Like many schools in this highland area, their biggest obstacle is sanitation. As mentioned, the current number of students has exceeded the construction scale and is expected to continue to increase, but the whole school has only one toilet for male and one for female.

Ms. Hien visits a boarding room for male students

According to Ms. Lieu, the previous scale invested in building the school was only for 200 children. Now, the number of students is up to 584 students. The rooms as well as the auxiliary works do not meet the demand. To have a place to live for the children, the school must convert the cafeteria into a living room and use the yard to make a place to eat, enough to cover the sun and rain.

The yard that used to be a place for children to play has now been used as a place for eating

Currently, teachers and students at Lao Chao Secondary School of Education and Training are gradually overcoming difficulties, and daily meals are also focused on by the school. The teachers organize for the children to grow vegetables, raise pigs, both to add to their daily meals and to practice life skills for themselves. It is known that the source of vegetables that teachers and students can grow themselves have provided 70% of the green vegetables of the kitchen, raising animals to improve meals for students or on holidays and Tet. On this occasion to visit the school, besides blankets, candies, warm clothes and plastic slippers for 584 students, Kien Vuong also contributes some essentials for the kitchen.

Ms. Lieu leads the group to visit the green vegetable garden for teachers and students to take care of every day.

We can't have enough strength to change the situation, overcome the difficulties for teachers and students, just hope to contribute with the teachers to "retain" the children to stay at the school to study properly, so that the students will be the ones to draw a bright future for themselves.

Leaving Lao Chai commune, on the way back across the road turning into Ta Ghenh village, we remember the image of teachers who come to class every day with their feet covered in mud. Then again, a contradictory concern arose: "Every time we visit a place that is really poor and difficult, we feel pity but at the same time we feel happy and sad, everyone understands, but we are glad that we find the right place, the right person, appear when they need us most. But saying that, many people think that we wish them to be poor."- Because of that thought, Mr. Qui and Ms. Hien always want to come to the places to know the actual situation, so that all the help can be brought with the most practical meaning.

en/kien-binh-minh-foundation-from-haptic.title: Kien Binh Minh Foundation: From “Haptic Eyes” to “Heart” – The journey bringing Tet to the High and remote places in Northwest region Vietnam
en/kien-binh-minh-foundation-from-haptic.body:
In 2014, on the journey to bring light to the visually impaired community, Kien Binh Minh Foundation used to stop in Lao Cai and this time back, we return and carry a different mission.

After more than 3 months have passed since the historic storm Yagi passed through and caused much loss and pain to the people of the northern provinces, of which Lao Cai was the most severely affected. The destination of the Kien Vuong company delegation this time was A Lu commune, an area that suffered the most damage in Lao Cai province. During the recent storm, the terrible landslide on September 9, 2024 in Phin Chai 2 village left 7 people dead and buried many houses. This time, the Kien Vuong company delegation was connected and accompanied in the program "Spring Border Guard Warms the Hearts of the Villagers" organized by the Lao Cai Provincial Border Guard Command.

A Lu is a highland commune in the northern mountainous region, with harsh natural conditions. The commune is considered one of the poorest localities in the district with the number of poor and near-poor households accounting for over 80%. The intellectual level of the people is still limited, so the problem of increasing income and improving people's lives is always a big challenge for the local government. The whole commune has 940 households, with 3 ethnic groups: Mong, Dao, Ha Nhi living together. The terrain is fragmented, with little arable land, and people's income mainly depends on growing corn and rice once a year.

Together with the local government, Kien Vuong Company has brought practical donations to help poor households affected by natural disasters and hopes to help them have a prosperous and fulfilling Tet holiday. In addition, Kien Vuong Company also supports poor students to overcome difficulties and excel in their studies.

The delegation of Kien Vuong company has donated 516 gifts including rice, seasoning powder, warm clothes, dried fish, and plastic containers of clean water to the households and awarded 30 gifts to good students who overcame difficulties in A Lu commune.

Another successful journey has come to an end, but it has opened a new voyage in 2025, extending the paths that bring Kien Vuong closer to the community. More than the "social responsibility" of a business, it is the empathy, sharing and spirit of mutual love that have become the "red thread" throughout the journey since the establishment of Kien Binh Minh Foundation. Not only when natural disasters and calamities occur, but we understand that somewhere on this S-shaped strip of land, every day there are still ethnic minorities in difficult circumstances, not having enough food and clothes; there are still children with bare feet covered in mud but constantly trying on the path to find knowledge; somewhere there are still rickety, makeshift schools, lacking in every way; there are still teachers who silently stick to school day and night, dedicating their youth to their homeland for a brighter future for the less fortunate children in remote border areas, the homeland's frontier... and much more.

Kien Vuong Company received a certificate of merit and a letter of thanks from the Lao Cai Provincial Border Guard Command

Kien Vuong Company is quite fortunate to receive support and companionship from the staff of Kien Vuong Company. Every small action of the staff of Kien Vuong Company is truly valuable and adds motivation to maintain and develop the Kien Binh Minh Foundation. Together with the strength of the community, Kien Vuong Company and Kien Binh Minh Foundation wish that all the people will have a prosperous and happy life, are supported to develop and enrich, all children go to school, no one is left behind, contributing to bringing the country into a new era - the era of national development.

en/revitalizing-mai-chau-children-to-sch.title: REVITALIZING MAI CHAU CHILDREN TO SCHOOL
en/revitalizing-mai-chau-children-to-sch.body:
Last April 22, after five months of returning from a visit to the children on the Dong Van Rock plateau in Ha Giang, the head of the Kien Vuong company was again able to visit a high school in Mai Chau District in Hoa Binh Province.

Most recent times in the media have covered stories of teachers from junior high school Tan Dan, Mai Chau District, Hoa Binh province to catch fish to supplement the meals for students. For the past six years, every time the students went to sleep , the teachers would close their books and go to the riverbed, wrestling with the waves and collect the fish shrimp. Tan Dan High School is located at the end of the 40km road from downtown Mai Chau, on the one side is the great mountains, on cloudy days, on the other side, a deep abyss with hundreds of twists and turns, up and down. On rainy days, the road into the school became more slippery and difficult to walk.

 The look in the beautiful kids from highland

Currently, the school has 116 students from Tay and Muong ethnic minorities studying. While the teachers are mainly Thai people, they have to learn more native languages to teach the children. This is also a common house for 62 childrens who are eligible for boarding, but the whole school only has 30 beds, so 2 children have to share one bed. The rickety boarding rooms are fitted with four old beds. Summer is hot and stuffy, winter is cold. In any room that doesn't have enough beds, students will lay sleeping mat on the floor. They have to do all their daily activities by themselves, from doing laundry to cooking. Worn notebooks are placed in cartons or wooden chests.

2 students sharing one bed

  

 The ceiling made for shelter from the rain and shelter from the sun is damaged

Mr. Ha Manh Quyet (Principal of Tan Dan Secondary School) said more, "According to the regulations on supporting students in remote areas, children whose houses are 7 km or more away from the school can be boarding and each child is provided with 460,000 VND per month for meals. Every week, the children will go home to get rice and some dry food to bring to school to organize their own cooking in groups". Thus, with this support level, each child will have about 15,000 VND/day for food. Suddenly, the Principal's voice suddenly dropped: "There are many children from very poor families, but their homes are less than 7km from the school and they are not supported, so they have to cross the forest and wade for dozens of kilometers every day to get to school.". The school is too far from the town center, and their family's situation is difficult, so they can't go and buy food and drinks. All food is provided by local people. Can't bear to see that the meals are too meager while the children are still in their adulthood, so the teachers decided to roll up their sleeves and go down to the lake every night to collect some fish and shrimp for the children to have a better meal.

It is the story of the poor lives of the highland children and the noble deeds of the teachers that inspire Mr. Qui and Ms. Hien to make this trip to Mai Chau.."I admired the teachers for their sacrifice and I am very touched when I saw them going to collect fish in the middle of the night for the children, so I decide to go and visit everyone." - Ms. Hien shares in a conversation with the teachers of Tan Dan Middle School.

The small hooves are the tools to collect fish every night of the "fishermen" of Tan Dan Secondary School. The slope from the school yard to the lake bed is steep and slippery, with stones and stones, just slip into the lake bed like playing.

As difficulties pile up, schools and classes are located close to the lake of Da River with dangers lurking. In order to ensure the safety of the children, the teachers often organize swimming lessons. Ms. Vi Thi Huong Giang (Vice Principal) confides with us: "The children here adapt very quickly, sometimes they can't even finish their studies before they swim like frog". That's how I see the strong vitality of the children who grow up from hardship. But even so, the teachers could not ignore the children for a single moment, because children who are playful and active are not aware of the danger. The teachers here not only teach the letters but also take care of and protect the children on behalf of their parents, sometimes when the children are sick, have a fever in the middle of the night, it is the teachers who are the ones who travel the dangerous roads to bring the children to the clinic.

Leaders of Kien Vuong company talk to Mr. Ha Manh Quyet and learn more about the difficulties that the school is facing

For several days in a row, it was hot, so the teachers took away the blankets, but the day the group arrived, it suddenly became cold and drizzly, so some children, whose health was not good, could not withstand the harsh weather. Seeing the children with fever, huddled in a thin blanket, and had no family to take care of, we couldn't help but feel sympathized. But the children, they don't know sad, lonely, only cry because they're tired, because they're in pain, because they can't receive gifts and play with their friends.

We don't want to call this charity, but just want to see it as a visit to the teachers and children and some gifts and to learn more about the situation here so that we can help more in a practical way. This time, Kien Vuong brings 213 gifts including clothes + winter coats for elementary students and 116 gifts of windbreakers + water bottles for middle school students. In addition, seasoning powder and cooking oil are divided equally between 2 schools to use for cooking for students. And of course, it is not fun for children if there are no candies.

Mr. Qui and Ms. Hien visit and give presents to the children of Tan Dan Primary and Secondary School

"Currently, with the help of sponsors, the school is gradually overcoming difficulties, thanks to everyone's hearts, the teachers here always want to dedicate themselves to the future of the children".  With sincere sharing from Mr. Quyet, Mr. Nguyen Huu Qui - Chairman of the Board of Director also expresses his wish: "Now we work together, we will share the burden with the teachers to help the children, but we not only give the children "fish" but also motivate them to "go fishing" by themselves.". After visiting and learning about the school's difficulties, Mr. Qui asks Mr. Bui Trung Dung (Director of Kien Vuong Hanoi) to coordinate more with Mr. Quyet to review the repair and build new beds so that the children would have a much safer and comfortable place to rest their backs and help them continue with their education.

If there is another opportunity to look back at the journeys that bring warmth to children in the highlands that Kien Vuong has gone through, from the far village of Nam Cha (Lai Chau), back to Lang Son, down to the top of the country, then back to Mai Chau (Hoa Binh), people will not be able to help but feel sad when they realize one thing: our compatriots still have many difficulties! But a good thing is that even in difficult times, there are the warmth, the love - which are more precious than gold; the noble love of the teachers of Tan Dan Secondary School will still spread to many schools in difficult places and awaken the loving heart in each of us.

en/the-journey-to-sprout-on-the-rock.title: THE JOURNEY TO SPROUT ON THE ROCK
en/the-journey-to-sprout-on-the-rock.body:
Somewhere on this S-shaped strip of land, there are still many miserable people that even if we have been there, we can only feel a part of their hardship. People often give the land of Ha Giang beautiful names such as "the beauty in the heart of the Tam Giac Mach flower season", "ideal adventure trails", "beautiful scenery as paradise"... but perhaps the ideal nature and beautiful paths are not meant for you - the hardy sprouts stretching on the rock.

Located about 130km north of Ha Giang city center, Lung Tao commune of Dong Van district is the residence of the H'Mong ethnic group. Lung Tao junior high school is nestled between the lofty cliffs on the Dong Van rocky plateau, where 52 teachers and 670 primary and secondary students teach and study. This visit to the school, Kien Vuong Company donates 20 dining tables, 140 plastic chairs, 16 conference tables along with gifts of warm clothes for all students and 305 preschool children. In addition, the group also donates 15 more winter blankets to the children who are boarding school at the school. On the day of the delegation's visit, Mr. Nguyen Van Thuan - Local Secretary and the Principal of Lung Tao Secondary School excitedly shares: "In the past, each meal had to be divided into 3 shifts to eat, but now there are more tables and chairs, so now only need one shift."

 The school is nestled between craggy cliffs

Small children go to school in thin clothes even when the weather gets cold

Có lẽ hoàn cảnh sống và điều kiện học tập còn nhiều khó khăn nên đã tôi luyện cho các em tinh thần tự lập rất cao. Những đứa trẻ ấy không biết vòi vĩnh bánh kẹo từ người lớn, tự giác thức dậy tập thể dục và vệ sinh sân trường vào mỗi sáng sớm mà không cần ai nhắc nhở."Bọn trẻ con ở đây rất thích đến trường vì ở trường các em vừa được học chữ lại được ăn no. Còn ở nhà chỉ toàn ăn ngô rồi phải lên nương phụ giúp gia đình" - chị Hằng cán bộ Tỉnh đoàn Hà Giang chia sẻ. Chúng tôi đã rất bất ngờ khi biết được cả trường chỉ có mỗi một nhà vệ sinh cho thầy cô và học sinh nên mọi người phải sắp xếp và phân chia ca để tắm giặt. Cô Nga - Phó hiệu trưởng trường bộc bạch: "Vì số lượng học sinh đông nên không phải em nào cũng được tắm rửa mỗi ngày, em nào đã tắm hôm nay thì ngày mai sẽ không được tắm". Còn có những thầy cô hằng ngày phải đến trường trên con đường đèo dốc quanh co một bên là vách đá cheo leo, một bên là vực sâu thăm thẳm. Những ngày trời mưa đường trơn trượt các cô còn phải quấn cả xích vào lốp xe để giữ an toàn.

Perhaps the living and learning conditions are still difficult, but it has trained the students to have a very high self-esteem. Those children do not know how to insist candy from adults. They wake up, exercise and clean up the school yard early every morning without anyone reminding them."The children here love to go to school because at school they can just learn to read and eat well. At home, I only eat corn and have to go to paddy fields to help out their family" - Ms. Hang, an official of Ha Giang Provincial Youth Union shares. We are surprised to learn that the whole school only has one toilet for teachers and students, so everyone has to arrange and divide shifts to shower and do wash. Ms. Nga, vice-principal of the school confides: "Because of the large number of students, not all of them can take shower every day. Those who have taken shower today will not take shower tomorrow." There are also teachers who have to go to school every day on a steep and winding road, one side is a cliff, the other is a deep abyss. On rainy days, the road is slippery, they even have to wrap the chain around the bike's tire to stay safe.

 No need reminder, the children always wake up very early and assign each other to clean the school yard

Although it is hard, those who have carried on their shoulders the mission of cultivating noble people are still determined to stick to school, to class, to work hard to find words for children who are only accustomed to the mountains and hills and paddy fields. Nothing is more beautiful than the image of teachers, who roll up their sleeves, going into the kitchen to cook for their students meager meals, giving them a thin blanket when the weather gets cold. The journey that awakens young souls and infuses them with the magical light of knowledge is full of difficulties and challenges, but very glorious. Although lacking in material things, it is still full in soul.

The idyllic and warm love of teachers and students in the village on the back of the mountain

Đến với Lũng Táo vào đúng dịp chào mừng ngày Nhà giáo Việt Nam 20/11, chúng tôi còn cảm nhận được tình thầy trò bình dị và ấm áp nơi bản xa, làm xoa dịu cả cái không khí se lạnh của mùa đông phương Bắc. Trong bài phát biểu tại buổi lễ, Chú Nguyễn Hữu Quí - Tổng Giám Đốc của chúng ta đã bày tỏ mong muốn được giúp cho trường và các em có chỗ ăn, chỗ ở ấm cúng hơn, góp sức để nhà ăn đã khang trang rồi thì sẽ nâng cao vấn đề vệ sinh an toàn thực phẩm. Ngoài ra, cô Nguyễn Thị Hòa - Giám đốc công ty TNHH An Châu - người bạn đồng hành của Kiến Vương trong chuyến đi lần này còn quyết định trao tặng thêm 10 phần quà đặc biệt cho 10 em có thành tích học tập tốt nhất, vâng lời thầy cô và cha mẹ vào dịp tổng kết cuối năm học.

Coming to Lung Tao on the occasion of celebrating Vietnamese Teachers' Day 20/11, we also feel the idyllic and warmth teacher-student relationship in this remote village, soothing the cold atmosphere of the Northern winter. In his speech at the ceremony, Mr. Nguyen Huu Quí - General Director of Kien Vuong Co., Ltd expresses his desire to help the school and the children to have a more cozy place to eat and stay, contributing to the spacious dining room and improve food safety. In addition, Ms. Nguyen Thi Hoa - Director of An Chau Company Limited - a companion of Kien Vuong Co., Ltd on this charity trip, also decides to give 10 more special gifts to 10 children with the best academic achievements and who follow the guidance of teachers and parents throughout the school academic year. 


Mr. Nguyen Huu Qui - General Director has a friendly conversation with the teachers and students of Lung Tao Secondary School

There are many ways for people to share love with their fellow human beings, but we have chosen a special path, with no bright flags and flowers, nor needing to beat drums and gongs, as long as the heart is shared and spread to the right people and to where it is needed the most. It's not just a responsibility to the community and society. Like Mr. Qui, Ms. Hien always thinks: "I go for the unfortunate, not for personal purposes nor for borrowing them to polish our name". Hopefully in the future there will be many more meaningful trips like this, more schools will be supported, although it is just a shirt and blanket, but we believe it will be a motivation to help the children feel safe going to school and reach to a better future. I would like to borrow the lyrics of the song "My teacher"(Co Giao Ban Em) sounded in the middle of the school yard that day to replace the expectation of a tomorrow "from the village where birds fly, in the sky full of spring sun".

en/sad-story-in-a-remote-commune.title: SAD STORY IN A REMOTE COMMUNE
en/sad-story-in-a-remote-commune.body:
What do you see after the past volunteer trips?

Is not seeing the things around that people call fame

Only see love when sharing each other pieces

Is it a piece of cake, a sweater or a pair of forest sandals

What do you see beside the cheers ?

It's a picture of a 5-year-old boy carrying his brother who is thirsty for milk

In that area, people must find ways for a proper meal ?

YES. That is right. The children in the highlands are still suffering!

In the winter, everyone's face is red

Not because of the hot sun, but because of the cold winter

So how many feet are worn with sandals ?

Even the children, the shirt is still tattered!

In the city, we keep running like chaos

Try going to the highlands and you will have tears in your eyes

Not because of the things that we always wonder

Just simply, there is not a piece of cucumber for the meal

Those are all the feelings that my brother and sister whenever we have the opportunity to participate in a charity trip in the highlands. It is the feeling of the heart tighten up when we see the unfortunate lives of the children, the feeling of extreme sadness when we accidentally hear a story about the plight of a few children here.

….

Ending the 7th day with mixed emotions. We return to the hotel to rest when the clock is already close to 12 o'clock at midnight. The next morning, the group gathers at 5:15 a.m. and take off to Loc Binh Town to attend the program. 

It's cold in the early morning in this mountainous area! Dense fog covered the whole space. The car drives in the cloudy weather with white fog. Everyone seems to be tired, but we also try to tease the driver with a sentence: Take care driver! We are about to close our eyes and take our soul to heaven! The whole car laughed for a while, then quieted down as everyone fell asleep... 

 

7h30…

The program begins with a series of successful activities. This time, Kien Vuong donates 200 sets of clothes, 200 warm coats and 200 pairs of slippers to children from elementary to high school with difficult circumstances. 

These gifts are planned to be divided and distributed to the three poorest communes in Lang Son province: Nhuong Ban commune, Yen Khoai commune, Xuan Man commune. Although the plan is like that, but because the program of the day is very long, so the organizers decide just go directly to Nhuong Ban commune to give gifts. The rest of the gifts will be sent to the members of the union members of Yen Khoai and Xuan Man communes to give to the children later.

It is about 30 kilometers from the place where the program is held to Nhuong Ban commune. This is one of the three most economically disadvantaged communes in the province. 100% of the San Chi ethnic group, they attach their life to the forest, clinging to the forest from the most difficult days.

Road to Nhuong Ban Commune

 

To get to the school site - the place where the awarding ceremony is held, the group has to walk and climb a very high slope

That day we have the opportunity to talk to Mr. Dau - Former Chairman of Na Pan Cooperative. We still remember vividly about 15 years ago, when this Nhuong Ban commune had no electricity, no roads, people did not know what to do for a living... Every year, only one crop of upland rice could be grown, "The grass is taller than the rice in every crop". Poverty continued to follow the pine hills planted just to get firewood. Every day, people carry firewood and walk for kilometers to the district market to sell, but the proceeds are only enough to buy salted butter and rice. He said: "since people planted pine trees to exploit plastic and wood processing, people's lives have gradually become more stable. But the children here are still suffering. Because in addition to lack of food, they also have to suffer the loss of their father because their father have to go to prison due to smuggling goods across the Border".

As soon as he finishes speaking, Mr. Dau turns to show us a little girl who is wiping her brother's face, about 2 years old, and then said: "Speaking of which, that's Beng. There are only two siblings at home. They live with their mothers! Their father has been arrested. Caught a long time ago. His father saw that his two children's stomachs were so hungry, his father listened to people's temptations, took risks, went to the Border to get goods to sell to evade taxes and was arrested. Now their mother is working in paddy field. She has to carry her younger brother to school with her." 

 

Two siblings of Phi Beng family

Approaching to talk to her, at first, this 6th grade girl is shy and do not want to talk to us. Only after Uncle Dau speaks, she begins to open up more.

Answering our questions fluently, Phi Beng shares: "Phi Lu ( the younger brother ) has been sleeping on my shoulder since Phi Lu was less than 1 year old. My mother has to go to the paddy fields, she has to work to buy rice for us to eat. When the house runs out of rice, my mother and I eat men men (a dish made from corn of the ethnic people)"

We ask them: "So when Phi Lu is under 1 year old but you have to carry him to school, how do you learn? How to take care of him?"

The sister answers: "Phi Lu is very good. In the morning, my mother cooks rice, drains the rice water into a bottle, and then gives it to Phi Lu to drink. When I go to class, I study, and Phi Lu just sleeps. When Phi Lu cries, I give Phi Lu rice water. When he cries too much, the teacher comforts him"

We ask: "So Phi Lu does not have milk to drink? And only when your mother goes to the District market to sell firewood and corn, that is when Phi Lu can drink milk ? ...And you? How about you? Do you find it difficult to study? Do studying and taking care of your brother at the same time affect your study ?"

The sister answer: "It's okay. I'm familiar with it. I have to learn the word! Learn to read so that you won't be lured like my father. My mother told me so"

Our heart shrink when we hear the innocent answers from Phi Beng. And Phi Lu just keeps staring at us. Probably because he feels strange. 

 

At a very young age, Phi Lu has to suffer too many disadvantages

Most of the children here, even though they are in 6th grade, are all small due to lack of nutrition

 

11:30 am…

The event ends. We get back into our to leave. Sitting in the car, going on the winding road, the car goes up and down again and again because the road surface is too bumpy and convex. We think, maybe only when the roads are more perfect, people's lives here can develop. Children like Phi Lu will have milk to drink, and Phi Bang will not have to bend over and carry her brother on her shoulders to go to school. But... will that thought ever come true?!

en/giveaway-program-back-to-the-highland.title: GIVEAWAY PROGRAM: BACK TO THE HIGHLANDS NAM PO
en/giveaway-program-back-to-the-highland.body:
After many days of preparation, the program "Back to the highlands" jointly organized by Kien Vuong and Dien Bien Provincial Youth Union was successfully held on October 28. As the information shared with the Kien family, this time Kien Vuong's destination is a highland border school in Dien Bien province - Vang Dan Primary School - Secondary School, Vang Dan Commune, Nam Po District .

The whole Vang Dan ETHNIC Primary and Secondary School has 900 primary and junior high school students, the school that KV gives gifts to in this phase is the learning place for 351 junior high school students, including 311 students. part-time birth. This spacious new school was built with the help of sponsors and a part of the local budget.

On this day 5 years ago, Kien Vuong visited Dien Bien and gave more than 1000 gifts to students at 3 schools: Quai Nua Secondary School, No. 1 Quai Nua Primary School and No. 2 Quai Nua Primary School, Tuan Giao district. This trip was still connected with the Provincial Youth Union and with the support of Nam Po District Youth Union so that the gifts could be given to disadvantaged students in Vang Dan commune. Besides 351 gifts including warm clothes, slippers, notebooks and candies, there are also 157 cotton blankets and 4 6000 liter water tanks to ensure the supply of clean water to the school.

Each person single-handedly distributes gifts in small portions before the program begins.

Welcoming the Kien Vuong delegation, besides teachers and students, there were also officials from Dien Bien Provincial Youth Union, Nam Po District Youth Union and soldiers from the border post.

 

The 4 water tanks given by Kien Vuong are used to store clean water from wells at the commune committee, about 1km from the school. From now on, teachers and students no longer have to worry about lack of water for daily living, cooking and washing.

Through the pictures above, it can be seen that the school's facilities are somewhat more spacious than most schools in the highlands that Kien Vuong has ever gone through. Perhaps this is also a good sign, showing that these children in the remote highlands still receive care and support from sponsors and benefactors. And Kien Vuong is still willing to contribute a part of his energy to join hands with the community to build upland children a fuller learning and development environment. Although we know that there are still many difficult schools, many children are still disadvantaged, but we still have faith that one day, not far away, there will be more solid schools built along this northern highland land. To make the path to find the children's letters less arduous. Farewell to Dien Bien, Kien Vuong's journey will continue to roll, wherever he needs help, both physically and mentally.

The vegetable garden is planted and cared for by the students themselves to improve their daily meals.

 

There is so much more to tell about the recent journey, see you all in another post to share more!

en/kien-vuong-awarded-20-scholarships-to.title: KIEN VUONG AWARDED 20 SCHOLARSHIPS TO "LIGHT UP DREAMS"
en/kien-vuong-awarded-20-scholarships-to.body:
On the occasion of Vietnamese Teachers' Day 20/11, Mr. Ta Van Vang - Deputy General Director and Mr. Nguyen Ngoc Son - Director of Finance and Accounting represented Kien Vuong Co., Ltd visited and awarded 20 scholarships to 20 students from Luu Huu Phuoc Primary School (District 8, Ho Chi Minh City) for showing that they can overcome difficulties and excel in their studies in the program "Lighting up dreams" with a total amount of 43,680,000 VND.

Representative of Kien Vuong Co., Ltd. awarded 20 scholarships to the students 

Hopefully, with the timely companionship and support from Kien Vuong Co., Ltd., this will help the student to have more motivation to overcome difficulties and strive to become diligent and become good students.

Also with the occasion of the beginning of Spring 2017, teachers and parents of Luu Huu Phuoc Primary School send a letter to thank and wish some meaningful wishes to Kien Vuong Company. This will be the motivation for Kien Vuong to continue on the journey to lighten up the dream for the family and many other less fortunate students in the near future.

en/journey-from-quài-nưa-to-vàng-đán.title: Journey ""FROM QUÀI NƯA TO……….. VÀNG ĐÁN""
en/journey-from-quài-nưa-to-vàng-đán.body:
Điện Biên - a land marking a heroic period of the nation with many historical relics and landscapes, this is also a Northwestern mountainous province with harsh tropical monsoon climate, influenced by the hot, dry west wind combined with the rugged terrain, mainly steep mountains that makes transportation inconvenient. 90% of the population here are ethnic minorities, and the lives here are very challenging.

So throughout the journey in all regions of the country, with the mutual mindset and desire to help share some with the minorities, Dien Bien is the destination where the Kien Vuong family carries out the charity program.


First visit in Quài Nưa, Tuần Giáo, Điện Biên:

In October 2017, amid the early days of the Northwest dry season, the Kien Vuong family had the opportunity to donate more than 1,000 gifts to students at 3 schools: Quài Nưa Secondary School, Quài Nưa Primary School No. 1 and Quài Nưa Primary School No. 2.

Ms. Nguyễn Thị Thu Hiền - representing the Board of Directors shared the responsibilities of Kien Vuong family to the community and society

 

The second visit to the border communes of Vàng Đán, Nậm Pồ, Điện Biên:

In October 2022, through the connection with the Dien Bien Provincial Youth Union, the Kien Vuong family had another busy volunteer program with the title "Returning to the Nạm Pồ highland" at Vàng Đán Primary - Secondary School for the Ethnic Minorities. At here, the Kien Vuong family donated 351 gifts and 04 stainless steel water tanks. In the border highlands, clean water scarcity often occurs, especially in the dry seasons, and many areas do not have clean water to use. The water supply from the streams depends mostly on the weather. Lack of water for daily uses often affects the student's living conditions.

Kien Vuong donated 04 stainless steel water tanks to the teachers and students at Vàng Đán Primary - Middle School for Ethnic Minorities

The young children of the Vàng Đán Primary and Secondary School are attentively watching the newly received gifts

Having traveled and learned a lot about the difficulties and hardships of people in the border areas, so after the meeting with Mr. Sùng A Phông - Captain of the reinforced border officer in Vàng Đán who understood very well what the people in the border areas lacked and what they needed to improve their living, immediately on November 15, 2022 and on January 5, 2023, the Kien Vuong family in all 4 regions of the country have joined hands together to prepare gift bags containing used good-quality clothes, carefully selected and thoroughly cleaned, along with shoes, books, and comic books, to give to the children.

Captain Sùng A Phông teaching the students of the Vang Dan Primary and Secondary School.

 

Kien Vuong Family providing the warm sports shoes to the young children

 

The villagers of Vàng Đán commune participating the “Zero-Dong Self-select Market” , organized by Kien Vuong Family with support from the border guards of Nà Bủng

 

Returning to Vang Dan in early Spring 2024 for the program “Accompanying Women and Children in border areas”:

Most recently, in March of 2024, with the collaboration between the officers of the Provincial Border Guard Command in Điện Biên and the Border Guard Post of Na Bung - individuals who consistently uphold the motto '3 cling, 4 together' (3 cling: Clinging to the working unit, clinging to the local, clinging to the guidelines and policies ( of the government ); 4 together: Eating together, living together, working together, and speaking the same language together) - the Kien Vuong family once again returned to the residents of Vàng Đán. Along the way to the donation area, we saw numerous inadequate houses. These houses were dilapidated over time, hot in the summer, leaky in the rainy season and cold in the winter.

A H'mông child in Huổi Khương village, Vàng Đán Commune stood curiously watching when we took photos

 

The house of a H'mông family in Lộc Cốc 2 village, Vàng Đán Commune, crudely built with bamboo and thatched grass

While hosting the program "Accompanying Women and Children in Border Areas in 2024”. We got the opportunity to experience the joy of the women and young children while they receiving the meaningful gifts. The difficulties in their daily lives were shown through the images of young children walking barefoot to school because they did not have shoes.

 

The young children of Vàng Đán Kindergarden receiving gifts

After carrying out multiple volunteer programs in Điện Biên. We have observed that the people in border areas were not only fellow countrymen, but also they were comrades who always stand side by side with the border guard soldiers to defend every inch of land, serving as “Border Markers” at the forefront of the nation's territory, contributing significantly to safeguarding national sovereignty. Ms. Nguyễn Thị Thu Hiền still concerned and hoped that in future the Kien Vuong Family could send more medicine, essential supplies, and basic household items to the people in more remote areas, where they were not fortunate enough to connect with the community.

May these wishes, though very simple yet full of love, not only from Ms. Hiền but also from the entire Kien Vuong family, continue to spread the values and share the hardships with the people of Điện Biên Highland.

Kien Vuong Family and the border units hosting the program "Accompanying border women and children"

en/300-gifts-handed-over-to-poor-childre.title: 300 GIFTS HANDED OVER TO POOR CHILDREN IN HA TINH
en/300-gifts-handed-over-to-poor-childre.body:
With the help of Ha Tinh Provincial Youth Union, on August 26, Kien Vuong comes and hands over 300 sets of clothes, 300 plastic slippers and 3000 notebooks and sweets to poor students overcoming difficulties in 2 districts Thach Ha and Can Loc belong to Ha Tinh province. In order for timely gifts to reach children in the new school year, it is thanks to the cooperation with local authorities to search for students with difficult circumstances but have the will to study and practice well. The overriding goal is still to make sure the help goes to the right place, to the right person, and really means something to the recipients.

Like many poor rural areas of Ha Tinh province, the buffalo and the paddy fields are the main occupation of the people of Thach Ha and Can Loc districts. However, nature is not favorable, the terrain is mountainous, dangerous, low plains, irrigation for agriculture is also very limited, crops rarely get a good year. Chance of losing is much higher than other districts of the province. The standard of living is therefore only enough to eat and clothe, but the education of children and grandchildren is always a top concern. Because they are always aware that if they cannot go to school, do not have a decent job, the solution for Ha Tinh youth is to leave their hometown to the city, or go to Cambodia or Laos and become illegal workers in foreign countries - which is what no one wants.

About 20km south of Ha Tinh city, the road leading to Thach Ha and Can Loc districts is flanked by hills with mountains on both sides, houses with red roofs, rows of green areca trees wrapped around, lush green fields, waiting for the harvest day. Appearing in front of our eyes is the appearance of a peaceful countryside in the new sun. A poor countryside but with many glorious victories in history, a poor countryside but is also hometown of many heroic people who have become legends.

Crossing the paved village road that cuts through small fields, the first destination of the delegation is the Hall of the Viet Xuyen Commune Committee, when the delegation arrived, although it is not yet time to start the gift giving program, all 150 children gathered under the guidance of the teachers. As soon as we see them, the first thing that pop into our mind is: the clothes and shoes that have been divided by age must be too big for them! The children at the age of "eat, sleep, and study well" like many of their peers, but their bodies are as tiny as candy sticks, plus the tanned, tough brown skin of children of central region, looks both lovely and heartwarming at the same time!

Tôi chợt nhớ đến những em bé trên cao nguyên đá Đồng Văn tí tuổi đầu đã biết địu em trên lưng để đỡ đần cha mẹ, mùa đông đến trường bằng đôi chân trần giữa cái rét cắt da cắt thịt, rồi những đứa trẻ ngày ngày lặn lội đến lớp trên con đường hiểm trở, một bên là vách núi cheo leo, một bên lòng hồ sông Đà sâu thẳm với bao nguy hiểm luôn rình rập. Tương lai các em rồi sẽ đi về đâu, các em không thể nào cứ trông đợi mãi vào tấm lòng của những nhà hảo tâm, không thể mặc mãi những chiếc áo ấm nghĩa tình...Nếu một ngày không còn sự san sẻ từ cộng đồng, liệu các em có còn đủ "sức" để tiếp tục đến trường? Đó cũng là điều mà Chú Quí và Cô Hiền trăn trở sau mỗi chuyến đi. Điều chúng ta mong đợi là ở sự bền vững và lâu dài, con cá phải đi liền với cần câu. Chúng ta không chỉ gánh vác giúp các em một phần khó khăn trong cuộc sống lúc này mà còn là tạo thêm động lực để chính các em tự mình "đi câu", tự vươn mình lên khỏi số phận, để làm giàu trên chính quê hương mình. Và thật ấm lòng khi nghe các em hào hứng nói về ước mơ sau này: "Ước mơ của con là gì? - Con muốn được làm thợ xây, để xây nhà cho mọi người ở!" Tôi dường như bắt gặp lại ánh mắt sáng ngời của cô bé dân tộc Mông khi nói về ước mơ được trở thành một cô giáo mầm non. Và chúng tôi muốn được chung tay cùng cộng đồng chắp cánh cho những ước mơ của các em bay cao, bay xa hơn nữa...

We suddenly remember the little children on the Dong Van rocky plateau who knows how to carry their children on their backs to support their parents, go to school with bare feet in winter, in the cold, and then the children every day, wading to class on a dangerous road, one side is craggy cliffs, one side is Da river lake with many dangers always lurking. Where will the children go in the future, they cannot wait forever in the hearts of benefactors, cannot wear warm clothes forever... If one day there is no more sharing from community, will they still have the "power" to continue going to school? That's also what Mr. Qui and Ms. Hien worry about after each trip. What we expect is the sustainability in long-term, the fish must go hand in hand with the rod. We not only put on our shoulder some of the difficulties in their lives at this time, but also create more motivation for them to "go fishing" by themselves, to rise above their fate and become more successful. And it is heartwarming to hear the children excitedly talk about their future dream: "What is your dream? - I want to be a constructor, to build houses for people to live in!" We seem to catch the bright eyes of a Hmong girl when talking about her dream of becoming a pre-school teacher. And we want to join hands with the community to give wings to their dreams to fly higher and further...

 When called to the podium to receive gifts, the children line up in an orderly manner without any fuss, or pushing each other

Mr. Nguyen Huu Qui - Chairman of the Board Directors representing the Company to give gifts to children at Viet Xuyen Primary School, Thach Ha District, Ha Tinh Province.

Farewell to the children in Viet Xuyen, the delegation continues to move to Thuong Loc primary school, Can Loc district to give the remaining 150 gifts. Welcoming the delegation is Mr. Phan Dinh Chinh - Principal of the school and the warmth and intimacy of many parents that exuded from the simple, seasoned faces of the people in the harsh central region.

The eyes and the tight fists of the people here will probably be the most memorable moments in the charity journey

Ms. Nguyen Thi Thu Hien - Vice Chairman of the Board of Directors expresses her desire to share with the children a difficult part in life, so that they can go to school with peace of mind for a brighter future.

Mr. Phan Dinh Chinh - Principal of Thuong Loc Primary School sends thanks to Kien Vuong company for giving them meaningful gifts before the new school year

Mr. Nguyen Khanh Trung - Deputy Director of Human Resources, Kien Vuong Company's Branch in Bac Ninh and the children of Thuong Loc Primary School

Mr. Nguyen Vu Toan - Deputy Director of Sales, Kien Vuong Company's branch in Bac Ninh giving gifts to poor students who overcoming difficulties in Can Loc district

Hopefully these small gifts will be a source of spiritual encouragement, contributing to sharing with thes students a difficult part in life, giving them more motivation to overcome the situation and move on to the upcoming new school next year

Leaving Thuong Loc Primary School, on the way back, the delegation goes to offer incense at Dong Loc T-junction relic - a place that has become a legend of the unparalleled wisdom and courage of Ha Tinh people, specifically, here places the youth volunteer force with a heroic fighting spirit, under the fierce rain of bombs and bullets day and night and help ensure the road and traffic to the front line. Thousands of heroes and martyrs fell down here, including 10 Dong Loc girls who died at a very young age.

Bringing smiles, the brightness of children's eyes, and the sacred atmosphere at Dong Loc T-junction marking a heroic history of the nation, we bid farewell to the heroic Ha Tinh land to return to the present, prepare for many next trips with more practical gifts, reaching many disadvantaged children across the country.
